g.data-narration: Welcome to Sampling Variables - the foundation of statistical inference that bridges samples to populations.
In computer science, we rarely have entire populations. Sampling lets us make reliable inferences from manageable data subsets.
A population is the complete data set, while a sample is a subset we actually observe and analyze.
g.text: Fixed unknown value (e.g., μ, σ, p)

g.data-narration: A sampling variable is a random variable representing all possible values a sample statistic can take across different samples.
The sampling distribution shows how sample means distribute across many samples, forming the basis for inference.
Standard Error measures the precision of our sample statistic. It is the standard deviation of the sampling distribution.
g.text: Effect of sample size on SE:

g.data-narration: The Central Limit Theorem states that regardless of population shape, the sampling distribution becomes Normal as n increases.
g.text: For large n (>30), the sampling distribution
of X̄ is approximately Normal regardless

g.data-narration: Watch how different population distributions transform into Normal sampling distributions as sample size increases.
g.text: CLT Visualization: Building the Normal Distribution
Different Population Shapes → Same Normal X̄ Distribution

g.data-narration: Let's apply these concepts to a real CS scenario: analyzing API response times with known population parameters.
Using the Z-score formula, we calculate the probability that sample mean exceeds a threshold despite known population mean.
g.text: Even with true μ=120ms, there's ~19% chance of observing X̄ > 125ms in a sample of 50

g.data-narration: These are the essential formulas for statistical inference: Standard Error, Z-score, and Confidence Interval calculations.
g.text: 💡 Remember: Larger n → Smaller SE → More Precise Estimates

g.data-narration: Sampling variables power real-world CS applications from ML model evaluation to business intelligence and quality control.
Sampling variables enable us to quantify uncertainty and make inferences from samples to populations, the foundation of statistical analysis.
g.text: Key Takeaway: Sampling Variables Bridge Sample Data to Population Inference
